landing.subtitle: MY MUSICAL MIND
landing.ctaSub: Explore the mind behind the engine

1.name: Dopamine Seeker
1.description: You chase the rush of synaptic transformation. The moment tension breaks into release—that is where you exist. Predictability is death; the drop is life.

6.name: Tension Architect
6.description: You are the master of the build-up. While others crave the destination, you understand that the journey—the careful layering of dissonance and suspension—is the true alchemy.

7.name: Contrast Addict
7.description: You live in the extremes. The whisper and the scream. You transmute silence into impact. For you, music without dynamic range is just flat noise.

18.name: Dramatic Arc
18.description: You perceive music as a transformative narrative. You don't hear notes; you hear scenes, conflicts, and resolutions. You turn sound into cinema.

2.name: Harmonic Purist
2.description: Consonance is your sacred geometry. You see the mathematical perfection in every interval. Chaos disturbs you; you seek the crystalline truth of perfect resolution.

4.name: Minimal Zen
4.description: In structure and repetition, you find infinite depth. While complex minds seek chaos, your mind constructs cathedrals out of silence and subtle variation.

5.name: Resolution Junkie
5.description: You have an addiction to closure. The perfect V-I cadence isn't just a sound; it's a physiological relief. You build mental structures that demand completion.

9.name: Pattern Hunter
9.description: You decode the blueprints of sound. Recurring motifs, hidden symmetries, inversions—your mind maps the architecture of a piece in real-time.

20.name: Precision Mind
20.description: Accuracy is your aesthetic. You hear the grid behind the music. Every deviation in pitch or time stands out like a flare. You seek the perfect execution.

3.name: Chaos Explorer
3.description: You thrive in the entropy. Where others hear noise, you hear new potential. Glitches, dissonance, and broken structures are your native language.

10.name: Sonic Nomad
10.description: A traveler of the sonic landscape. You refuse to settle in one genre or timbre. Your mind demands constant novelty; familiarity is stagnation.

19.name: Curious Wanderer
19.description: Your listening is a question, not an answer. You explore sound with innocent curiosity, unburdened by the need for predefined structures.

23.name: Edge Runner
23.description: You live on the boundary of what is considered 'music'. You push your perception to the edge, finding beauty in the breakdown of the signal.

24.name: Renaissance Mind
24.description: A rare anomaly. You belong to no single family because you embody them all. You have high tolerance for chaos AND high craving for structure. A universal explorer.

8.name: Structural Romantic
8.description: You feel the architecture of emotion. Music weaves a story that anchors your memories. For you, a melody is a thread to the past.

11.name: Emotional Anchor
11.description: Your cognition is guided by the heart. You don't analyze music; you feel it resonate in your chest. Emotional truth is your only metric.

13.name: Tonal Dreamer
13.description: You drift in the textures and colors of sound. Gravity has no hold on you. You anchor yourself in the atmosphere rather than the beat.

15.name: Quiet Observer
15.description: Silence speaks to you. You find your center in the spaces between notes. A deep, meditative listener who anchors the world in calm.

17.name: Ambient Flow
17.description: You exist in a state of flow. Time dissolves into texture. You anchor yourself in the present moment, washed over by sound.

22.name: Nostalgic Soul
22.description: Your mind is a library of the past on fire. Music is the key to unlocking forgotten times. You anchor your identity in the soundtrack of your history.

12.name: Rhythmic Pulse
12.description: You don't just hear rhythm; your neurons fire in sync with it. Movement, groove, and pulse are the fundamental languages of your mind.

14.name: Dynamic Storm
14.description: A hurricane of cognition. You crave high energy, high density, high impact. Your mind creates order out of the eye of the storm.

16.name: Groove Mechanic
16.description: You feel the micro-timing that makes a head nod. The Pocket. You understand the mechanics of motion and the subtle friction that creates groove.

21.name: Raw Energy
21.description: Subtlety is fine, but power is truth. You respond to the visceral impact of sound waves hitting the body. Music is a physical force.

sections.comingSoon: Narrative coming soon — M³ is still learning this persona's story.

infoHub.title: The Engine & The Minds
infoHub.subtitle: Explore the architecture behind musical cognition and the 24 persona archetypes it reveals.
infoHub.engineDescription: M³ is built on three layers of musical intelligence: R³ captures 97 dimensions of perceptual reality, H³ tracks temporal morphology across 32 horizons, and C³ maintains 5 cognitive beliefs that model how your mind experiences music.
infoHub.personaAtlasSubtitle: Each persona represents a unique region in the cognitive parameter space. No mind is better or worse — only different.

transcendent.name: Transcendent

monologue.reward: Your sense of musical pleasure shifted +{{delta}}% this week. That's significant — your mind is recalibrating what "good music" means to you. The songs that moved you last month might not hit the same way next month.
monologue.familiarity: Something shifted in how you recognize music this week. Your memory response climbed +{{delta}}% — you're building deeper connections with the music you hear, finding new layers in songs you already know.
monologue.tempo: You felt rhythm differently this week. Your rhythmic engagement surged +{{delta}}% — your body is locking into more complex beats, anticipating groove patterns before they drop.
monologue.salience: Your attention to musical detail went quiet this week. Surprise sensitivity dropped {{delta}}% — you were listening inward, soaking things in rather than reacting to every twist. That's depth, not disengagement.
monologue.consonance: Your sense of harmony shifted {{delta}}% this week. The way you hear chords and intervals is changing — melodies that once felt neutral are starting to carry emotional weight.
monologue.tensionHigh: With your love of tension at {{pct}}%, you live for that moment right before the music resolves. This week's mix of Electronic and Post-Rock fed that craving perfectly — long builds, delayed payoffs, maximum anticipation.
monologue.monotonyHigh: Your comfort with repetition ({{pct}}%) is a superpower most listeners don't have. This week's ambient sessions let you sink deeper into patterns without getting bored — each loop revealed something new.
monologue.entropyHigh: Chaos appetite at {{pct}}% — you thrive on the unexpected. The genre diversity this week kept your curiosity satisfied without burning you out. You need variety like breathing.
monologue.resolutionDefault: Your need for closure ({{pct}}%) shaped your week. Every resolved chord, every satisfying ending — your mind marked each one as a small victory. You hear music as a series of promises kept.

consonance.title: {{genre}} shaped how you hear harmony
consonance.body: Your sense of harmony peaked at {{val}} — you were deeply tuned into chord progressions and intervals. {{tracks}} tracks through your {{family}} ears.

tempo.title: Your body locked onto {{genre}} rhythms
tempo.body: Rhythmic engagement hit {{val}} across {{mins}} minutes. You were moving with the music more than usual — anticipating beats before they landed.

salience.title: A high-attention listening session
salience.body: Your attention peaked at {{val}} — {{tracks}} moments grabbed you. {{genre}} tends to hit your {{persona}} mind's surprise triggers hard.

familiarity.title: You went deeper into what you know
familiarity.body: Memory response climbed to {{val}}. You're building stronger connections with familiar sounds — hearing structure where you used to hear surface.

reward.title: Peak pleasure — this is what flow sounds like
reward.body: Musical pleasure reached {{val}}. Everything aligned: enough surprise to keep you engaged, enough familiarity to feel right. Your mind was in the zone.

evolution.consonance: Your sense of harmony is {{dir}} ({{start}} → {{end}}). Chords and intervals that once felt neutral are starting to carry more emotional weight for you.
evolution.tempo: Your rhythmic feel is {{dir}} ({{start}} → {{end}}). You're building richer internal timing — anticipating groove changes before they happen.
evolution.salience: Your surprise sensitivity is {{dir}} ({{start}} → {{end}}). You're getting better at filtering noise from signal — responding to genuinely important musical moments.
evolution.familiarity: Your musical memory is {{dir}} ({{start}} → {{end}}). Patterns click faster now — what took 3 listens before registers on the first play.
evolution.reward: Your pleasure response is {{dir}} ({{start}} → {{end}}). Your mind is getting more selective — it takes more nuanced music to produce the same rush.

recommendations.resolutionReason: You love when music resolves beautifully. This piece teases you with delayed endings — building anticipation then delivering exactly the closure your mind craves.
recommendations.resolutionPE: Delayed resolution → deeper satisfaction when it arrives
recommendations.tensionReason: 7 minutes of slowly building intensity before the release at 4:32. Your love of tension means you'll ride the anticipation like a wave — and the payoff is worth every second.
recommendations.tensionPE: Long build → massive release at the climax
recommendations.entropyReason: Your chaos appetite can handle the unexpected rhythms. Each micro-disruption will spark your curiosity without overwhelming you — the perfect edge between confusion and discovery.
recommendations.monotonyReason: Phase-shifting patterns that evolve so slowly you barely notice — until you realize you're somewhere completely new. With your repetition comfort at {{pct}}%, this will feel like a meditation, not a loop.
recommendations.monotonyPE: Gradual evolution → deep immersion without boredom
recommendations.salienceReason: The viola entry at 4:05 cuts through 3 minutes of quiet tension. Your mind will flag it as THE moment — the one event in the whole piece that redefines everything before it.

similar.connection0: You and {{name}} hear music through remarkably similar ears. When they hear an unexpected chord change, their reaction is nearly identical to yours — same surprise, same satisfaction.
similar.connection1: Both minds share a {{family}} love for {{trait}}. You pick up on the same details in a song — the same moments grab both of you.
similar.connection2: Your musical minds are so aligned that you'd probably love each other's playlists. You reach the emotional peak at the same moments in a piece.
similar.connection3: You both experience time in music the same way — short moments feel equally intense, long passages build the same way. Shared listening would feel natural.
similar.divergence: {{axis}} is where you differ most — that's what makes shared listening interesting, not boring.

Alchemists: You're not chasing the drop. You're chasing the moment everything you expected shatters — and something better takes its place.
The tension you crave isn't stress. It's your mind building something complex enough to be worth the resolution.
Every unresolved chord is a question you refuse to leave unanswered. That's not anxiety — that's hunger.
Architects: You don't just hear music. You hear the blueprint behind it. Every deviation from the pattern is information.
Your mind builds a running model of every song — and the pleasure comes from watching it prove itself right.
Harmony isn't beauty to you. It's confirmation that the universe has structure and you can hear it.
Explorers: Your curiosity runs hotter than most. You need the unknown like oxygen — silence is just an unexplored frequency.
When you hear something completely new, you don't flinch. You lean in. That's rarer than you think.
Your mind keeps its expectations deliberately loose — so more surprise gets through, more learning happens, more joy arrives.
Anchors: Your mind wraps sound in memory. Every melody is a key to a room you've already been in — but see differently each time.
Repetition doesn't bore you. It deepens. Each listen carves the groove a little wider, a little richer.
You listen with your entire history. Each note lights up a chain of memories that most people never build.
Kineticists: Your body doesn't just respond to rhythm — it predicts it. You feel the beat before it lands.
The groove is a precision instrument in your mind. Tiny timing shifts that others miss register as completely different textures.
You lock onto rhythmic structures with unusual speed — you're moving before most people have even found the beat.

trackContext.high: Your mind lit up here — the surprise-to-satisfaction ratio was {{ratio}}x above your baseline. This is one of those rare moments where everything clicks.
trackContext.mid: Strong response. Something in this section caught your attention and held it — a sustained wave of enjoyment that built slowly and paid off.
trackContext.low: Steady-state listening. Your mind was comfortable here — familiar territory, low surprise, the kind of music you can sink into without thinking.

compatNarrative.high: Your auditory cortices share an almost identical processing architecture. When this {{persona}} mind hears music, their prediction-error cascade mirrors yours within 0.08σ. The {{axis}} axis alignment is remarkable — you'd reach peak reward at the same musical moments.
compatNarrative.good: Strong resonance in {{closestAxis}} processing — your temporal windows align. The {{furthestAxis}} divergence creates complementary tension: where your mind rests, theirs explores, and vice versa. This is the kind of neural asymmetry that makes shared listening transformative.
compatNarrative.mid: Complementary architecture. Your {{closestAxis}} systems share common ground, but the {{furthestAxis}} gap means you hear fundamentally different structures in the same piece. This divergence isn't weakness — it's the basis for perspective expansion.
compatNarrative.low: Contrasting minds. Your prediction models operate on different wavelengths — what triggers your reward system leaves theirs indifferent, and vice versa. But contrast is where growth lives: exposure to this mind's listening patterns could unlock new perceptual dimensions for you.

insights.mockPE: Jon Hopkins — 'Luminous Beings' at 4:32 — the bass drop completely blindsided your sense of harmony while the track already felt deeply familiar. That contrast — surprise inside comfort — triggered a 3.2x pleasure spike.
insights.mockDrift: You're getting more selective about what moves you. Your sense of musical memory is growing — patterns click faster. And this week's rhythmic engagement jumped, probably from the Electronic/Post-Rock mix.

dashboard.sharedTraits: Your auditory cortices share {{count}} processing traits.
dashboard.chatInitial: Hey! I saw we matched at {{pct}}%. What's your peak track been this week?

phases.p1: Listening to the sound — breaking it into 128 frequency layers
phases.p2: Reading 97 perceptual features from every moment
phases.p3: Measuring harmony: how rough, how pure, how tonal...
phases.p4: Looking at patterns across time — from milliseconds to minutes
phases.p5: Tracking speed, direction, and rhythmic repetition...
phases.p6: Your mind is predicting what comes next
phases.p7: Comparing predictions to reality — where were you surprised?
phases.p8: Updating beliefs — how confident is your mind about each dimension?
phases.p9: Computing pleasure: surprise + resolution + exploration − boredom
phases.p10: Integrating across 8 time scales — from beats to movements

narratives.consonanceHigh: You were deeply tuned into the harmony here. Chord changes hit you strongly — resolved intervals felt satisfying, and dissonance created real tension you wanted resolved.
narratives.consonanceMid: You processed the harmony with flexibility — both consonant and dissonant passages felt natural to you. You're comfortable with harmonic ambiguity.
narratives.consonanceLow: Harmony wasn't the main thing driving you here. You let chords wash over you without needing them to resolve — you hear structure through other channels.
narratives.tempoHigh: Your body locked onto the rhythm. You were tightly synced with the beat — anticipating tempo changes and moving with the groove instinctively.
narratives.tempoMid: You tracked the rhythm without being dominated by it. Complex rhythmic passages didn't throw you off — you're flexible enough to handle polyrhythm.
narratives.tempoLow: Rhythm wasn't your primary channel here. You experienced this music more as texture and color than pulse and beat.
narratives.salienceHigh: Your attention was grabbed hard. Sudden changes in dynamics, texture, or melody triggered strong reactions — your mind was on high alert for the unexpected.
narratives.salienceMid: Balanced attention. You noticed structural changes without being overwhelmed by them — your filter separated the important moments from the noise.
narratives.salienceLow: You experienced this as a smooth flow. Rather than reacting to individual moments, you let the music carry you as a continuous stream.
narratives.familiarityHigh: Your memory kicked in strongly. You were recognizing patterns, connecting themes across the piece — each repetition deepened your understanding rather than boring you.
narratives.familiarityMid: Patterns emerged gradually. Your mind was slowly building connections, but each repetition still felt somewhat fresh — you hadn't fully mapped the structure yet.
narratives.familiarityLow: This felt new throughout. Your pattern recognition kept the novelty signal high — even on re-hearing, it would likely feel relatively fresh.
narratives.rewardHigh: Strong pleasure response ({{value}}% average). Surprises resolved into satisfaction — moments of "I didn't expect that" followed by "but I love it."
narratives.rewardMid: Selective pleasure ({{value}}% average). Your enjoyment spiked at specific moments rather than being sustained throughout — quality over quantity.
narratives.rewardLow: Reserved response ({{value}}% average). Your standards are high — it takes more nuanced or unexpected music to trigger real satisfaction.

entropyTolerance.detail: One embraces chaos, the other seeks order. Find the shared frequency where both minds resonate.

resolutionCraving.detail: One craves closure, the other resists. Navigate the tension and find where both feel complete.

monotonyTolerance.detail: One thrives in loops, the other needs novelty. Build a bridge between patience and exploration.

salienceSensitivity.detail: Your attention peaks differ. Synchronize your salience — notice the same moments together.

tensionAppetite.detail: One builds tension higher, the other seeks release sooner. Find the alchemical balance point.

entropyTolerance.Alchemists: Channel chaos into transformation — let dissonance fuel your alchemy
entropyTolerance.Architects: Use controlled chaos to stress-test your structures
entropyTolerance.Explorers: Push deeper into the noise — your edge is where others retreat
entropyTolerance.Anchors: Let chaotic textures color your emotional palette
entropyTolerance.Kineticists: Layer polyrhythmic chaos over your groove foundation

resolutionCraving.Alchemists: Maximize the payoff — build longer arcs before resolution
resolutionCraving.Architects: Perfect your cadence sensitivity with complex harmonic progressions
resolutionCraving.Explorers: Use resolution as a surprise tool in unexpected contexts
resolutionCraving.Anchors: Deepen emotional satisfaction through delayed gratification
resolutionCraving.Kineticists: Find rhythmic resolution in polymetric convergence points

monotonyTolerance.Alchemists: Find transformation within repetition — the loop evolves you
monotonyTolerance.Architects: Hear the micro-variations that hide inside repetition
monotonyTolerance.Explorers: Discover how repetition creates its own kind of novelty
monotonyTolerance.Anchors: Let familiar patterns anchor you deeper into feeling
monotonyTolerance.Kineticists: Lock into the groove — repetition is where pocket lives

salienceSensitivity.Alchemists: Amplify your sensitivity to dramatic peaks and drops
salienceSensitivity.Architects: Sharpen your detection of structural turning points
salienceSensitivity.Explorers: Train your ear to catch the most novel moments instantly
salienceSensitivity.Anchors: Attune to the emotional weight of key musical moments
salienceSensitivity.Kineticists: Push your sensitivity with dense polyrhythmic passages

tensionAppetite.Alchemists: You are the master of tension — push build-ups even further
tensionAppetite.Architects: Engineer longer suspension arcs with more complex layering
tensionAppetite.Explorers: Seek tension in unfamiliar harmonic territories
tensionAppetite.Anchors: Let tension deepen your emotional investment in the music
tensionAppetite.Kineticists: Build rhythmic tension through accelerating density

entropyTolerance.Alchemists: Sit with unresolved chaos longer before seeking the drop
entropyTolerance.Architects: Try free-form listening without seeking patterns
entropyTolerance.Explorers: You already thrive here — explore chaos in unfamiliar genres
entropyTolerance.Anchors: Gradually introduce noisy textures into your comfort zone
entropyTolerance.Kineticists: Listen to broken-beat and glitch to expand rhythmic chaos

resolutionCraving.Alchemists: Practice leaving tension unresolved — embrace the open ending
resolutionCraving.Architects: Try ambient pieces that avoid traditional cadences
resolutionCraving.Explorers: Let go of needing an ending — some journeys don't close
resolutionCraving.Anchors: Explore music that fades rather than resolves
resolutionCraving.Kineticists: Listen to asymmetric rhythms that never fully land

monotonyTolerance.Alchemists: Build patience with minimal techno and loop-based music
monotonyTolerance.Architects: Study Steve Reich — repetition as architectural material
monotonyTolerance.Explorers: Challenge yourself with long drone pieces
monotonyTolerance.Anchors: Sit with a single repeated melody and notice what shifts
monotonyTolerance.Kineticists: Try meditative drum loops to build rhythmic patience

salienceSensitivity.Alchemists: Practice noticing subtle shifts, not just the obvious peaks
salienceSensitivity.Architects: Listen for quiet structural changes in ambient music
salienceSensitivity.Explorers: Slow down and attend to detail within familiar territory
salienceSensitivity.Anchors: Expand your attention beyond emotional peaks to texture
salienceSensitivity.Kineticists: Train with dynamic pieces that reward patient attention

tensionAppetite.Alchemists: Explore music with slow, gentle tension curves
tensionAppetite.Architects: Try pieces that build tension subtly over long durations
tensionAppetite.Explorers: Let tension accumulate naturally instead of seeking it
tensionAppetite.Anchors: Gradually increase tolerance with cinematic build-ups
tensionAppetite.Kineticists: Listen to progressive pieces that build energy slowly
